edge_hypothesis: MACD 1h is a proven winner: 6:1=PF 1.460 (ALL regimes profitable), 7:1=PF 1.712 (ACCEPT). The 4h MACD curve peaked at 7:1 and declined at 8:1 (PF=1.442) and 12:1 (PF=1.302). But the 1h timeframe may have a DIFFERENT optimal R:R than 4h due to: (1) 4x more bars = more granular entries, (2) shorter holding period = less regime exposure per trade, (3) different noise characteristics on 1h vs 4h. We need 5:1 and 8:1 on 1h to determine if the 1h curve mirrors 4h (peak at 7:1) or diverges. Advisory directive #17 (TEST_MACD_7TO1_ON_1H) is now answered — 7:1 on 1h matches 4h champion PF. The remaining question: where does the 1h curve actually peak? If 8:1 on 1h holds near PF=1.7, the 1h curve peaks higher than 4h (which declined to 1.442 at 8:1). This would make 1h the preferred MACD timeframe at higher R:R ratios. Additionally, 5:1 on 1h tests the moderate end — if PF > 1.35 (matching 4h moderate at 5:1=1.353), it confirms the full 1h R:R curve sits above 4h due to more signals. Invalidation: if all 1h variants cluster near PF=1.4-1.7 regardless of R:R, the 1h signal quality is uniformly mediocre and the R:R curve is flat (no optimization possible).

variants.name: macd_1h_tail_harvester_5to1
variants.description: MACD confirmation on ETH/1h with 5:1 R:R tail harvester exits. Stop 1.5x ATR, TP 7.5x ATR. The moderate end of the 1h R:R curve. On 4h, 5:1 achieved PF=1.353 (ACCEPT). With 1h's higher trade frequency, this should produce 200+ trades (vs 139 on 4h), providing excellent statistical confidence. If PF exceeds the 4h moderate variant, it confirms 1h as the superior timeframe for MACD across the board. Expected win rate: 25-35% (higher than 7:1 due to closer TP).

variants.name: macd_1h_tail_harvester_8to1
variants.description: MACD confirmation on ETH/1h with 8:1 R:R tail harvester exits. Stop 1.5x ATR, TP 12.0x ATR. The critical comparison: on 4h, 8:1 declined to PF=1.442 (from 7:1's 1.712). Does the same decline happen on 1h? If 1h at 8:1 stays near PF=1.7, the 1h timeframe has a flatter R:R curve (robust to R:R changes) — making it more reliable for live deployment where slippage shifts effective R:R. If 8:1 drops sharply on 1h too, it confirms 7:1 as the universal MACD optimum regardless of timeframe.

variants.name: macd_1h_tail_harvester_9to1
variants.description: MACD confirmation on ETH/1h with 9:1 R:R tail harvester exits. Stop 1.5x ATR, TP 13.5x ATR. The extreme end of the 1h curve. On 4h, 12:1 achieved PF=1.302 — deep decline from 7:1's 1.712. On 1h, if 9:1 stays above PF=1.4, it confirms that the 1h curve is flatter than 4h (declines more slowly with R:R). This would mean 1h MACD is more tolerant of R:R variation — crucial for live trading where effective R:R shifts due to slippage and fees. Expected: 150-170 trades (similar to 7:1 since trade count is entry-driven, not exit-driven), PF range 1.2-1.5 if the 1h curve is flatter than 4h.
